title: Israel is using the ceasefire in Gaza to cement its grip on the territory
subhead: Nearly eight months after Israel announced a ceasefire that its army is violating daily, the country continues to strike the part of the enclave it occupies. Some in Israel are calling to resume airstrikes more intensively.

title: France's Kouamé, 17, becomes youngest man into Grand Slam third round since Nadal
What is France's 'Black Code' that MPs finally voted to repeal?
'Putin and Xi have an ace up their sleeve named Trump'
subhead: In his second term, the US president has confirmed his intention to distance the country from the 'Western collective.' Beijing and Moscow are both hoping to overturn the global order with Trump's help, writes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

title: Anti-Bolloré controversy could leave the entire French film industry worse off
subhead: The uproar between Canal+, the leading body funding French cinema, and the signatories of an open letter against Vincent Bolloré, Canal+'s owner, came at the worst possible time: in the middle of the Cannes Film Festival and as crucial agreements for the sector are up for negotiation.

title: In Brittany, luxury tourism is taking over the coastline
subhead: In Saint-Malo, a court granted approval at the end of March for the construction of a 4- and 5-star hotel complex and a thalassotherapy center on the site of a former municipal campground. This luxury project is one of many springing up across the region.

title: Political scientist Robert Kagan: 'War in Iran risks permanently weakening the position of the US in the world'
subhead: The foreign policy specialist, closely linked to neoconservative circles, warns in an interview with Le Monde that the conflict with Iran over control of the Strait of Hormuz could mark a global turning point.

subhead: In their new book, journalists Jean-Michel Décugis, Nicolas-Charles Torrent and Le Monde's Jérémie Pham-Lê recount the events of the burglary at the Paris museum on October 19, 2025, and the arrest of the suspects. Here are excerpts from the book.
byline: Jean-Michel Décugis,  Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Nicolas-Charles Torrent

title: Kyiv attacks: The complex logistics network behind Russia's war machine
subhead: The Ukrainian capital endured a barrage of strikes over the weekend, including ballistic missiles, cruise missiles and long-range drones. These increasingly frequent hybrid operations reveal the intensification of Russian drone and missile production.

title: French banker lands exceptional and controversial contract to restructure Venezuelan debt
subhead: Matthieu Pigasse was tapped by Caracas to negotiate the restructuring of Venezuela's massive debt. His competitors suspect Washington played a role.
